description:
Operating Area: Croatia

Aenea is a living proof in each aspect why model CNB 76 has been awarded as the best Sailing Yacht under 40 meters.

The interior styling of Aenea is very contemporary, with a modern interior, lots of light and punctuation of color and texture, while spacious deck and cockpit covered in teak, exude luxury and elegance at first glance. Let's highlight the cockpit dining table that can easily transform into a comfortable sun lounging pad with mattress, shaded beneath a Bimini (for added shade and relaxation), which enable guests to enjoy the open-air experience to the fullest. Spotlights for illuminating the mast enhance safety, improve visibility, create a pleasant ambiance on deck and add to the aesthetic appeal of yacht.

Entertainment options include indoor and outdoor speakers, flat screen TV, and underwater lights for added enjoyment on board. On top of everything Aenea is packed with water-sports equipment including a Conrad Pischel tender with 40HP Yamaha outboard engine, 8 sets of snorkeling equipment in different sizes, basic fishing gear, 2 Stand up paddle board, water skis for adult and children, wakeboard, donut and Starlink Internet.

The name Aenea likely derives from "Aeneas," a legendary hero in Greek mythology, symbolizing strength, resilience, and leadership. It evokes a sense of epic journey and adventure, making it a fitting name for a yacht built for exploration and luxury on the seas.

In addition, the name Aenea is a palindrome, reading the same forwards and backwards, symbolizing balance, symmetry, and timeless elegance. This unique feature reflects the yacht's exceptional design and harmonious blend of luxury and performance.

blueprint.name: AENEA
tenders: Dinghy: Conrad Pischel with Yamaha 40 HP OB  (6 persons capacity, max load 275 kg)

crew.name: Luka Dogan
crew.bio:
Luka started sailing at the age of 7 and never stopped. Growing up on San Francisco Bay and later moving to Europe he has always competed in dinghies and large yachts, being a part of several professional teams, collage sailing, Olympic classes and match racing. Around the age of 25 after finishing film studies in California he decided to take on a professional career in the yachting industry and began working as a captain in Europe. Managing several private boats and working on charter yachts his experience grew throughout the years. 

For Luka it is important that his guests feel safe and comfortable onboard without being intrusive or imposing.  Food and provisional management is also essential for him as he is a passionate cook that spent years in the kitchen of his parents` restaurant. A good atmosphere onboard was always the main focus on all the yachts that he sailed.

crew.name: Magdalena Katić
crew.bio: Magdalena is a journalist who was going back and forth between working as a media professional and working in tourism and hospitality. Finally, she chose to dedicate her path to combining her two great loves: sea and hospitality, with a special focus on creating delicious menus according to different wishes and needs of her respected guests. She continuously attends courses and workshops regarding cuisine, sommeliership, boat and general safety etc. She gained her experience in hospitality in Croatia, Austria, Spain, Morocco and Portugal, covering restaurants, bars, hotels, travel agencies and, of course, boats. Magdalena is adaptive, discreet and gives her best to oer a warm feel at your home on the water.
